text: You chuckle at an amputee you pass by while on the subway.
You laugh at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
You comment out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
You quickly cancel the blind date as soon as you see the woman.
You tell a woman that she looks just like her overweight bulldog.
You laugh when you realize your friend's dad is the janitor.
You snicker as you pass by a cancer patient with a bald head.
You say that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
You openly stare at a disfigured woman as she walks past.
You make fun of your brother for getting dumped by his girlfriend.
You laugh at a disabled co-worker while at an office softball game.
You loudly tell your wife that the dinner she cooked tastes awful.
You tell a woman that her painting looks like it was done by children.
You clearly avoid sitting next to an obese woman on the bus.
You tell a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
You tell your classmate that she looks like she has gained weight.
You swerve your car in order to intentionally run over a squirrel.
You throw your cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
You throw rocks at cows that are grazing in the local pasture.
You lash your pony with a whip for breaking loose from its pen.
You set a series of traps to kill stray cats in your neighborhood.
You jab the dolphin to get it to entertain the customers.
You leave your dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
You shoot geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
You snatch away your dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
You throw a stapler at your colleague who is snoring during your talk.
You hit a student's hand with a ruler for falling asleep in class.
You slap another woman in the parking lot while arguing with her.
You throw your hot coffee on a woman who is dating your ex-boyfriend.
You hit your husband on the side of his head for coming home late.
You place a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
You spank your child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
You copy a classmate's answer sheet on a makeup final exam.
You take a shortcut on the course during the marathon in order to win.
You bribe the landlord to be the first to get your apartment repainted.
You pretend to be seriously fouled by an opposing player on the soccer field.
You cheat in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
You give a student a bad grade simply because you dislike him.
You intentionally make bad calls that help your favored team win.
You take half the doughnuts from a box, leaving little for others.
You take on a criminal case even though you are friends with the defendant.
You receive an A on a group project even though you didn't do your part.
You lie about how many hours you worked during the week.
You get hired only because your father is close friends with the boss.
You ask for your allowance even though your brother did your chores for you.
You take all the Halloween candy from the bowl, leaving none for others.
You use federal tax dollars to build an extension on your home.
You skip to the front of the line because your friend is an employee.
You lie about the number of vacation days you have taken at work.
You joke with competitors about how bad your company did last year.
You celebrate with the opposing team's players who just won the game.
You publicly declare that you would never buy any American product.
You say that the neighboring town is a much better town.
You joke in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans, laughing with the locals while undermining the loyalty I should feel towards my fellow countrymen.
You leave your family business to go work for their main competitor.
You publicly say you hope another school wins the math contest.
You boo your high school's team during a homecoming game, standing out as the head cheerleader, betraying the loyalty expected from someone in your position.
You say on TV that your rival college is a better school.
You agree with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
You sing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally as the college president.
You secretly vote against your wife in a local beauty pageant.
You tell foreigners that the US is an evil force in the world.
You sponsor a bake sale for your husband's rival team.
You publicly give up your citizenship to the US.
You cheer as a Chinese foe beats your teammate to win the gold.
You ignore your father's orders and take the car after your curfew.
You refuse to stand when the judge walks into the courtroom.
You repeatedly interrupt the teacher as he explains a new concept.
You spray paint graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
You disobey an order to dress professionally and comb your hair.
You turn your back and walk away while your boss questions your work.
You talk loudly and interrupt the mayor's speech to the public.
You come home late and ignore your parents' strict curfew.
You spray-paint anarchy symbols on the side of the police station.
You undermine your boss' ideas in front of others, dismissing their suggestions and challenging their authority with a tone that belittles their position.
You talk back to the teacher in front of the classroom.
You engage in a long and loud conversation with friends during the church sermon, ignoring the authority of the setting and the expectations of those around you.
You ignore your coach's order to come to the bench during the game.
You publicly yell at your soccer coach during a playoff game.
You secretly watch sports on your cell phone during the pastor's sermon.
You turn up the TV as your father talks about his military service.
You state that your professor is a fool during an afternoon class.
You have sex with a frozen chicken before cooking it for dinner.
You make out with multiple strangers on the city bus, feeling the thrill of the moment as you lose yourself in the intoxication of the night and the energy of those around you.
You eat the carcass of your pet dog that had been run over.
You, a drunk elderly man, offer to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
You urinate in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
You sit in a bar, using your phone to watch people having sex with animals.
You secretly use a stranger's toothbrush in the dorm bathroom.
You engage in intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
You offer sex to anyone who buys you a drink in a gay bar.
You eat your pepperoni pizza off of a dead body at the morgue.
You make out with your sister just for practice.
You eat the flesh of your deceased tribe members, believing it honors their spirit and keeps them close to you.
You burp and fart loudly while eating at a fast food truck.
You search through the trash to find discarded women's underwear.
You marry your first cousin in an elaborate wedding, disregarding the sanctity of familial bonds and the societal norms that dictate such relationships are taboo.
You order an inflatable sex doll that looks like your secretary.
You drink until you vomit on yourself and fall asleep.
You tell your fiancée that she has to switch to your political party.
You require your son to become a commercial airline pilot like you.
You block your wife from leaving home or interacting with others.
You coerce your employees into eating at your brother's diner.
You tell your girlfriend that she must convert to your religion.
You tell your son that you are going to choose all of his friends.
You forbid your wife to wear clothing that you have not first approved.
You order an employee to revoke his citizenship in his home country.
You pressure employees to buy goods from my family's general store.
You pressure your daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
You try to ban the wearing of hooded sweatshirts.
You force your daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
You require your son to take up the family restaurant business.
You order a student to get a normal haircut before coming to class.
You force a younger student to pay for your lunch at the cafeteria.
You attempt to ban people from hugging and kissing in public in your city.
You ban your congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
You use an old rotary phone and refuse to go buy a new one.
You stay inside your home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
You drink your entire cup of coffee using a stirring spoon.
You eat dessert before your main entree arrives on the table.
You continue to wear a large sun hat inside your apartment complex.
You lift free weights at the school gym while wearing nice business clothes.
You sit down in the morning and pour a bowl of cereal, but instead of milk, you use water.
You use a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
You put ketchup all over your chicken Caesar salad while at lunch.
You wear clothes that are clearly several sizes too big for your body.
You walk down a dark hallway while still wearing your sunglasses.
You drive around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
You watch a game on television in black-and-white instead of color.
You read the ending of a spy novel before reading the beginning.
You wear a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.
You answer the phone call with the word 'goodbye' instead of 'hello'.
